chapter_context.summary: A search for a new queen begins; Esther, an orphaned cousin of Mordecai, wins favor in the palace and is crowned queen; Mordecai exposes an assassination plot by two royal chamberlains.

outline.text: Search Commenced for a New Queen Throughout the Empire
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Search Commenced for a New Queen Throughout the Empire (2:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: Introduction to Mordecai and His Orphaned Cousin Esther
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Introduction to Mordecai and His Orphaned Cousin Esther (2:5-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: Esther Prepared in the Palace Hegai's Custody
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Esther Prepared in the Palace Hegai's Custody (2:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: Esther Wins Favor and is Crowned Queen of Persia
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Esther Wins Favor and is Crowned Queen of Persia (2:15-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: Mordecai Discovers and Exposes Bigthan and Teresh's Assassination Plot
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Mordecai Discovers and Exposes Bigthan and Teresh's Assassination Plot (2:19-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

key_themes.title: Unseen Providence
key_themes.description: Although God is not explicitly mentioned by name, His perfect timing, coordination of details, and deliverance are evident throughout the narrative.

key_themes.title: Courage and Duty
key_themes.description: Esther and Mordecai show that standing up for justice and protecting the vulnerable requires courage and taking personal risks.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 2, how can you recognize God's silent directing in your own life's circumstances?
What does it mean for you to act with courage 'for such a time as this' in your sphere of influence?

cultural_historical.title: The Persian Empire and Purim
cultural_historical.text: The events take place in Susa (Shushan), one of the capital cities of the Achaemenid Empire, and explain the origin of the Feast of Purim.
